message:
fix(channel): cancellation-aware partition sends and fault buffer ownership safety

Two related correctness fixes for the channel subsystem:

1. FaultSender::send() — The auto-flush error path previously cloned the
   value and returned it wrapped in SendError, but flush() already
   re-queues undelivered messages (including the just-sent one).  Returning
   ownership to the caller while the same value sits in the reorder buffer
   creates a duplication hazard on caller retry.  Auto-flush is now
   best-effort (let _ = ...) since flush handles its own re-queuing.
   Added test: auto_flush_cancelled_keeps_message_buffered_without_erroring_send.

2. PartitionSender::send() — Added early cx.checkpoint() cancellation
   guard so that partitioned channels under Drop or Error behavior respect
   task cancellation before routing the message.  Without this, a cancelled
   task could still silently drop messages (Drop mode) or report a
   disconnect error (Error mode) instead of the correct Cancelled error.
   Added tests for both Drop and Error partition modes under cancellation.

Also extracted test_cx_with_budget() helper for test Cx construction with
explicit budgets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
